skill.name: decisions
skill.description: Load and review stored architectural decision records before choosing a new technical direction.
skill.summary: Helps teams align new architecture choices with prior decision records.
skill.author: Clouder0

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backtick execution and weak cryptography, but review found no executable code or cryptographic operations. The backticks are Markdown fences around a Glob example and JSON examples, and line 3 is a plain description. No prompt injection, network access, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Interpreted as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick pattern surrounds a Glob tool example, not Ruby or shell command execution. The skill only declares Read and Glob as allowed tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file shows a Markdown fenced block containing Glob("memory/knowledge/decisions/*.json"). There is no Ruby syntax, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction.

low_findings.title: False Positive: JSON Examples Interpreted as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The reported backtick patterns surround JSON examples for recording and formatting decisions. They do not execute commands or invoke external programs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code fences are marked as JSON and contain structured decision record examples. No command interpreter, subprocess call, or executable script is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alert Has No Evidence
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm location is the skill description. It contains no hash function, encryption API, or cryptographic instruction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 only describes loading past architectural decisions. No cryptographic term, algorithm, or security-sensitive implementation appears at that location.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when new technical choices ignore prior architectural decisions. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to inspect decision records before recommending a path.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when architectural decision records should be consulted.
Directs the assistant to search memory/knowledge/decisions for JSON decision files.
Defines the expected fields for stored decision records.
Encourages following prior decisions or documenting justified divergence.
Provides a compact JSON format for recording new decisions.
limitations: Does not create, migrate, or validate decision files automatically.
Only covers decision records stored under the documented memory path.
Does not evaluate architecture quality without relevant project context.
Requires readable decision records to produce useful guidance.

use_cases.title: Align a New Architecture Choice
use_cases.description: Review existing decision records before choosing a database, framework, service boundary, or deployment pattern.

use_cases.title: Understand Existing Patterns
use_cases.description: Check why a project follows a current implementation pattern before changing it.

use_cases.title: Document a Technical Decision
use_cases.description: Capture context, options, rationale, and consequences after a team selects an approach.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review before making a change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the decisions skill to check whether prior architecture decisions exist for this change. Summarize the relevant records before recommending next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Options Against History
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the decisions skill to compare these options with existing decision records: [option A] and [option B]. Explain which prior decisions support or conflict with each option.

prompt_templates.title: Document a New Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the decisions skill format to draft a new decision record for this choice: [decision]. Include context, options, rationale, status, and consequences.

prompt_templates.title: Assess a Deliberate Divergence
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review when changing an established pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the decisions skill to find prior decisions related to [topic]. Identify where my proposed change diverges, then draft a concise justification for the divergence.

output_examples.input: Should we add a second message queue to this service?
output_examples.output: The assistant would first look for related decision records, summarize any queue or integration decisions, then recommend following or revisiting them.

output_examples.input: We chose Postgres over a document database. Help record why.
output_examples.output: The assistant would produce a decision summary with context, considered options, chosen approach, rationale, and expected consequences.

output_examples.input: Can we replace the existing API gateway pattern?
output_examples.output: The assistant would review prior gateway decisions, list conflicts with the proposed change, and outline what must be documented before diverging.

best_practices: Consult decision records before recommending architecture changes.
Document both the selected option and the rejected alternatives.
Record consequences so future teams understand the tradeoffs.
anti_patterns: Do not ignore existing records because a new approach seems faster.
Do not record only the final decision without context or rationale.
Do not treat outdated decisions as permanent without documenting replacement.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps assistants review and respect stored architectural decision records before making new technical recommendations.

faq.question: Where does it expect decision records?
faq.answer: It points to decision records under memory/knowledge/decisions as JSON files.

faq.question: Does it run shell commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill declares Read and Glob tools and includes Markdown examples, not executable shell scripts.

faq.question: Can it write decision files automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides a format for recording decisions, but writing depends on the host assistant and available tools.

faq.question: Who should use it?
faq.answer: Architects, engineering leads, and developers who need consistent technical choices across a project.

faq.question: Can it replace architecture review?
faq.answer: No. It improves context for review, but humans should still validate tradeoffs and organizational impact.
